steps: The specialist charges $160 for the first 2 hours. For 6 hours total, there are 6 - 2 = 4 additional hours beyond the base.
The additional cost: $360 - $160 = $200 for those 4 hours, so the hourly rate is $200 \div 4 = $50 per hour.
explanation:
• Choice A — '$f(x)=50x+60$': f(6) = 50(6) + 60 = 360. Derived from the base fee of $160 minus the 2 hours already included at $50 each, giving y-intercept 60. ✓
  • Choice B — '$f(x)=50x+160$': f(6) = 50(6) + 160 = 460 ≠ 360. Incorrectly counts the $160 base on top of all x hours at $50, double-counting the first 2 hours. ✗
  • Choice C — '$f(x)=60x$': f(6) = 360 coincidentally matches, but f(2) = 120 ≠ 160. This ignores the flat $160 base charge for the first 2 hours. ✗
  • Choice D — '$f(x)=60x+160$': f(6) = 60(6) + 160 = 520 ≠ 360. Both the rate and intercept are wrong. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
